steps.title: Identify the vertex
steps.content:
The function $f(x)=x^2+4$ is the basic parabola $y=x^2$ shifted up $4$ units, so its vertex is
$$(0,4)$$

steps.title: Identify the opening direction
steps.content: The coefficient of $x^2$ is positive, so the parabola opens upward. Therefore, the vertex is the minimum point of the graph.

steps.title: Match the graph
steps.content: The correct graph is the upward-opening parabola with vertex $(0,4)$. The answer is D.
